1.premise: A criminal father and a little-person stepdad produce a childhood with no one to look up to in either sense.
"Look up to" can mean admire or physically look upward at.

2.premise: A little-person stepdad could be mistaken for an older child.

3.premise: Bullies threatening a mother implies an insult, but reveals a future stepdad.

lines.text: I've been going to therapy lately.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Had a weird situation.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My real father was a criminal,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and my stepdad was a little person.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So I had no one to look up to.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Yeah.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: What?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: A lot of comics in the middle of the day go,
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: "Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait."
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: Go ahead, go.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: - When I met him, I was just five years old
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and he was just a little bigger than me.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And my mom was like, "This is your new stepdad."
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I was like, "My stepdad is seven years old."
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: And it gave me weird confidence 'cause he used to hit me.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Yeah, and even when I was little,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I was like, "This bigger kid hits like a bitch."
lines.label: tag

lines.text: But what really messed me up is I met him
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I was five.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So I just started school that year,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and I was getting bullied at recess.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: So I was like, "Wow, those kids really meant it
lines.label: setup

lines.text: when they said they were gonna fuck my mom."
lines.label: punchline
